name: Asthma
summary: Chronic inflammatory airway disease characterized by variable, usually reversible airflow obstruction, airway hyperresponsiveness, mucus hypersecretion, and progressive airway remodeling driven largely by Th2/IgE/eosinophilic pathways.
learningGoals: Recognize the clinical features and trigger pattern of asthma.
Relate asthma symptoms to airflow obstruction, airway hyperresponsiveness, and inflammation.
Explain early-phase and late-phase asthma pathophysiology.
Interpret obstructive pulmonary function findings and bronchodilator reversibility.
Compare rescue bronchodilators, controller therapy, and add-on therapies.
Connect receptor-level pharmacology to bronchodilation and anti-inflammatory control.
Identify important adverse effects and contraindications of common asthma medications.
Distinguish acute exacerbation management from chronic maintenance treatment.
